scope: ACL 2027 supplement page 13 grounding-derivation companion v3 visual iteration

motivation.target_page_before: After the v2 pass, page 13 still had a compact grounding companion and remained visually thin in the full supplement density audit.
motivation.goal_alignment: Add more registered render material to the derivation spread while keeping AI-generated schematic content exposition-only and avoiding figure-only or caption-only spill pages.

measurement.layout_guard_active_definition: RGB pixels where max(channel)-min(channel) > 15 or mean(channel) < 238, matching tests/test_paper_layout.py figure-density checks
measurement.page_active245_definition: RGB pixels where max(channel)-min(channel) > 15 or mean(channel) < 245, used for sparse-page raster review
measurement.red_pixel_fraction_definition: RGB pixels where R > 150, G < 90, and B < 90

claim_boundary.paper_use: visual reading guide for the point-in-box and normalized-1000 coordinate derivations
claim_boundary.notes: The v2 AI-generated metric-contract gate is not a metric, model prediction, benchmark row, VLM output, or new experimental result. Evidence-bearing panels remain the registered original/noMDL GRScenes target-view render pairs and deterministic protocol overlays.

metric_contract_gate_v2.role: AI-generated metric-contract schematic only; deterministic figure code draws critical labels, boundary text, and all evidence-source annotations.

final_figure.local_visual_evidence: The standalone v3 figure adds a denser registered coordinate-conversion render wall below the formula-to-render audit strip. The zoom-019 tiles are desaturated to avoid red fallback-like surfaces while preserving the registered render-pair role.

pdf_review.local_visual_evidence: Rendered page 13 keeps C.3-C.6 text, the larger grounding companion, caption, and the start of the grounding equations on one page. Page 15 closes with the navigation companion and metric-boundary bridge; the previous caption-only spill page is gone. Page 16 is the next supplement section rather than a derivation spill.
pdf_review.residual_risk: Page 14 remains formula-only and low density by the active-pixel audit, but it is a continuous derivation text page rather than a stranded figure or caption page.

layout_iterations.change: Moved the grounding companion before the Point-in-Box Grounding subsection and removed the hard clearpage that followed it.
layout_iterations.result: Selected: page 13 now carries the larger render companion in context; page 13 active245 rises to 0.134736802 at 90 dpi.

layout_iterations.change: Shrank the downstream metric-boundary bridge include width to 0.86 textwidth.
layout_iterations.result: Selected: the supplement returns to 45 A4 pages and the metric-boundary caption no longer spills to a near-empty page.

tests.result: RED first for missing v3 source/order expectations; GREEN after implementation: 2 passed, 72 deselected.
completed; regenerated fig_supplement_grounding_derivation_companion.png from the v3 composer.
completed during layout review; paper/venues/acl27/build/supplement.pdf contains 45 A4 pages.
completed; pages 12-16 regenerated for local visual review.
completed; pages 12-16 regenerated for density review.

tests.command: python paper/shared/figures/gen_supplement_task_media_atlases.py && python -m pytest -q tests/test_paper_layout.py && make -C paper acl27-supplement
tests.result: fresh final verification completed; figure generator regenerated supplement figures, tests reported 74 passed, and make built paper/venues/acl27/build/supplement.pdf.

normalized text scan found the required grounding companion, coordinate-conversion render wall, AI exposition boundary, and no-new-metrics boundary phrases; local-path and author-name leakage checks passed.
exit 0; no whitespace errors in tracked touched files.

tests.command: direct final-newline and trailing-whitespace scan over the new v3 doc, v3 evidence JSON, and touched text files
tests.result: exit 0; all scanned files end with a final newline and have no trailing whitespace.

review_mode.reason: Local pure visual review was used; no separate clean-room subagent was dispatched because the available subagent tool requires an explicit user request for delegation.
